close_reason: Completed in db8d88b with passing package lint/test/typecheck.
description:
Add visible execution mode labels to all worker-facing UI surfaces so operators can always tell whether a worker is running in current-branch or worktree mode.

## What to implement

1. **`/bw workers` display** — show `[current-branch]` or `[worktree]` badge per worker
2. **TUI dashboard worker list** — execution mode column or badge
3. **Worker detail view** — show `executionMode`, `checkoutPath`, `branchName`, `launchHead` (for current-branch); show `worktreePath` for worktree
4. **Statusline** — show active mode indicator when workers are running
5. **`/bw status`** — show default execution mode from config summary
6. **Worker notices** — include mode in launch/completion/attention notifications
7. **Aggregate views** — when mixed workers exist, group counts by mode (e.g., "3 current-branch running, 1 worktree held")

## Key UI Patterns

Use `isSuccessfulTerminalWorker(worker)` from `.1.3` for displaying success states (shows ✓ for both `verified` and `landed`). Use `worker.executionMode` for badge text. Use `worker.checkoutPath` for path display (works for both modes without narrowing).

## Where to look in existing code

- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/worker-diagnostics.ts` — worker detail formatting
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tui/worker-manager.ts` — TUI worker dashboard
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/statusline.ts` — statusline indicators
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/index.ts` — worker notices in `buildWorkerNotice()`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/actions/issues.ts` — `/bw workers` command output

## Design Rules

- Labels should be concise but unambiguous
- Use consistent terminology: "current-branch" and "worktree" (not abbreviations or alternatives)
- Do NOT hide the execution mode — it should always be visible in worker context
- For aggregate displays (counts), group by mode if mixed workers exist
- Detail view for current-branch workers should show `launchHead` (useful for debugging attribution)
- Detail view for worktree workers should show `worktreePath` (existing behavior)
- Use the `checkoutPath` field (available on both modes) for generic path display

## Tests

- Worker list shows `[current-branch]` badge for current-branch workers
- Worker list shows `[worktree]` badge for worktree workers
- Mixed-mode worker lists show both modes with correct badges
- Detail view shows mode-specific fields (`launchHead` for current-branch, `worktreePath` for worktree)
- Aggregate counts correctly group by mode when mixed workers exist
- `/bw status` displays the configured default execution mode
- Worker notices mention execution mode
- Statusline includes mode indicator

## Definition of Done

- All worker-facing UI surfaces show execution mode
- Consistent terminology used everywhere
- Mixed-mode scenarios display correctly
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean

comments.text: Implemented worker execution mode labels across /bw workers, TUI dashboard/manager, worker detail, statusline, /bw status, worker notices, and aggregate summaries. Added mode-label regression coverage for badges, mixed counts, detail fields, statusline, and status config. Gates: npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension; npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension; npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension.

Remediated review blockers for db8d88b: lifecycle notices now include explicit [worktree]/[current-branch] labels, /bw land success and queued-retry notifications include mode labels, and regression coverage was added for worktree/current-branch notices. Gates run clean: npm run lint/test/typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension. Commit e79063a.

title: Add UI/status labels showing worker execution mode
